source_pr.title: infoschema: stabalize TestSelectClusterTable test
source_pr.body:
### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/33294

Problem Summary:

The infoschema tests are unstable. This is caused by the HOST portion of the processlist now including the peer port (from https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/pull/30183 ). The solution is just to not include this in the results.

### What is changed and how it works?

Fixed tests only.

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- bugfix or new feature needs a release note -->

```release-note
None
```

failure_signature: The infoschema tests are unstable. This is caused by the HOST portion of the processlist now including the peer port (from https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/pull/30183 ). The solution is just to not include this in the results.
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky around DDL/schema changes where schema propagation is asynchronous. This PR stabilizes it by: infoschema: stabalize TestSelectClusterTable test.

evidence: evidence: The infoschema tests are unstable. This is caused by the HOST portion of the processlist now including the peer port (from https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/pull/30183 ). The solution is just to not include this in the results.
